channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pence said he congratulated Trump on his win at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said he congratulated President-elect Trump on his upcoming return to the White House when the two shared a brief greeting at former President Jimmy Carter’s funeral service on Thursday. “He greeted me when he came down the aisle. I stood up, extended my hand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Flight cancellations piled up and state officials warned of continuing dangerous roads Saturday in the wake of a winter storm that closed schools and disrupted travel across parts of the southern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: MAP: Emergency room visits for respiratory illnesses are soaring in these states
item.description: A number of states are seeing a spike in hospital visits caused by respiratory illnesses as we enter the New Year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: It was a week of fire and ice. It began with millions of people across the U.S. shivering amid blizzard conditions and frigid air that lasted for days, thanks to a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: An increasing number of Democrats are questioning whether their party’s commitment to diverse candidates, especially women, may lead to more political struggles in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Flames were already attacking the campus of Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center when the cantor, Ruth Berman Harris, and three companions rushed in to rescue its sacred Torah scrolls.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass rejects assistance from FDNY; Newsom accepts help from Mexico crews
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass recently rejected an offer for assistance from the New York Fire Department, outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Earlier this week, as hurricane-strength winds blew through bone-dry hillside subdivisions, Los Angeles saw its worst nightmare realized as long-predicted firestorms engulfed wide swaths of the nation's second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt for defaming Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge on Friday held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt for skirting an agreement not to make new false claims about two Georgia election workers he was ordered to pay $146 million for defaming.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 18 migrants found stowed in tanker truck near San Antonio
item.description: A group of 18 migrants were found inside a tanker truck by Texas law enforcement hundreds of miles from the border in Guadalupe County, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Woman who confronted Gavin Newsom speaks to NewsNation
item.description: Rachel Darvish, a mother from Pacific Palisades, is currently living in a hotel room with her daughter.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Praying in burning street 'was instinct': California fire survivor posts viral video in fiery embers
item.description: A Minnesota storm chaser recently went viral on social media for a video he posted praying over a fiery street during the Palisades Fire in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brooklyn real estate magnate pleads guilty to conspiring to funnel illegal donations to NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Brooklyn real estate magnate pleaded guilty Friday to working with a Turkish government official to funnel illegal campaign contributions to New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wildfires latest: 153,000 residents still under evacuation as Kenneth Fire order lifted
item.description: Firefighters are hoping for a break Friday from the fierce winds that have fueled massive blazes in the Los Angeles area, killing 10 people, obliterating whole neighborhoods and setting the nation’s second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant accused in attempted teen kidnapping deported 5 times: officials
item.description: A Mexican illegal immigrant deported from the United States five times has been arrested after allegedly attempting to kidnap a teenage Alabama girl on New Year's Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Letter from Gov. Cox calls for immediate removal of Salt Lake City ICE director
item.description: Gov. Cox signed a letter requesting changes to local immigration standards and the immediate removal of the ICE director in Salt Lake City.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WGN 9

item.title: Virginia mother shocked by burial of murdered daughter
item.description: Saunders sent photos of holes in the dirt on the gravesite, and one of them exposed the corner of her daughter's purple casket.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: California fire victim dies holding hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Photos capture wildfires ravaging Southern California
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Distracted semi-truck driver slams into another semi on side of Oklahoma highway
item.description: The Oklahoma Highway Patrol (OHP) is reminding drivers to give their full attention to the road after a dash cam caught one semi-truck crash into another on the side of the highway.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: CHICAGO — Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9
